situation_summary.primary_threat_axis: Multi-axis attack. Cruise missiles approaching from NW (Valbrek corridor) and central (Arktholm direct). High-altitude fighter on central axis. UAV swarm from NE. Decoy on western flank.

track_breakdown.notes: Original wave-1 track. Now close to zone. If not intercepted, imminent breach.
New wave-2 fighter. High altitude, central axis, high confidence. Likely strike lead.
Cruise missile on direct heading to Arktholm. Low altitude complicates intercept.
Cruise missile heading toward Valbrek industrial zone. Very low altitude.
Original wave-1 fighter. Approaching zone. Needs engagement decision.
UAV swarm on eastern approach. May threaten Nordvik or act as saturation element.
Original UAV. Still slow, low confidence. Deprioritized.
Suspected decoy on western flank. Low confidence. Do not allocate high-value assets.

anomalies: Attack pattern suggests coordinated multi-domain operation: fighters for air superiority, cruise missiles for ground targets, UAV swarm for saturation, decoy for distraction.
Resources committed to wave 1 are now unavailable. Total available force significantly reduced.
Cruise missiles at very low altitude may exploit radar shadows near Central Highlands.
situation_summary.confidence_assessment: High confidence in overall threat picture. Multi-axis attack confirmed. Resource pressure is the critical factor — available force cannot cover all axes simultaneously. Prioritization is essential.
situation_summary.force_status_note: Assets committed to wave 1 response are engaged and unavailable for reallocation. Reserve posture degraded.

narration: Second wave confirmed — five new tracks across three axes. A high-altitude fighter and a cruise missile are heading directly for Arktholm. Another cruise missile is targeting the Valbrek industrial zone at very low altitude. A UAV swarm is approaching from the east toward Nordvik. With assets committed to the first wave, our available force is significantly reduced. This is a multi-domain coordinated attack. Immediate re-planning is critical — we cannot cover all axes with remaining resources.
